verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It is synthesized as a preproenzyme, processed through the secretory pathway, and released as a soluble extracellular protein — it has no transmembrane domain, no GPI-anchor signal, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. Its primary biology is activation of PSA (KLK3) in prostatic secretions/seminal plasma. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets the protein body on intact cells; (2) not a TM precursor shed by sheddases — it is fully secreted with no membrane-anchored stage; (3) no documented wash-resistant TM-partner tethering; (4) no documented PM minority pool via cycling or dual localization; (5) no gene-specific evidence of cancer/cell-death/activation-induced outer-leaflet display, no germline-restricted surface, not a lysosomal TM protein. Diagnostic programs (serum hK2 assay) target the soluble secreted form. Verdict: secreted_only.
